page: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an x86_64 architecture server within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, powered by an AMD EPYC processor. The system features 24 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It is configured with a shared CPU allocation model. Memory capacity is 192.0 GB, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 3840 GB NVMe SSD. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and does not contain any graphics processing units.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance characteristics across multiple vectors. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable highlight, with large-block read-only operations reaching 458.63 GB/sec and write operations reaching 223.24 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% tier. PassMark CPU testing yields a strong score of 51919.27, supported by a physics score of 8367.75. Standard processing tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform within the average range. Database and web serving operations are also moderate, with Redis SET operations executing at 54.61 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 19.47 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers high memory and storage capacity relative to its compute footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. However, the shared CPU allocation model introduces potential performance variability under sustained, high-utilization compute loads. The combination of 192.0 GB of RAM, top-tier memory bandwidth, and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage makes this configuration well-suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, memory-mapped data processing, and moderate-scale web serving or backend application hosting.
description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an x86_64 server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, equipped with an AMD EPYC processor featuring 24 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, offering a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Performance benchmarks show strong CPU capabilities, with stress-ng scores in the top 25% and top-tier memory bandwidth exceeding 458 GB/sec for large-block reads. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained maximum compute, the high memory density and fast local NVMe storage make this server highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for databases, caching systems, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 24 shared vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd features 24 shared AMD vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3.8 TB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 24 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM
3840 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth exceeding 458 GB/sec for large-block reads
Strong CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Optimized for databases, caching layers, and memory-mapped workloads
